title: Today's AI Brief: Building self-improving tax agents with Codex
summary: A look at AI's latest moves: self-improving tax agents and Slackbot's AI upgrade.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're exploring a couple of interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence. First up, OpenAI, Thrive, and Crete have teamed up to build a self-improving tax agent using Codex. This new tool aims to automate tax filings, improve accuracy, and speed up workflows. I mean, who doesn't love the idea of making tax season a little less painful?

The self-improving tax agent is quite fascinating. By leveraging Codex, the team has created a system that can learn and adapt over time, potentially reducing errors and freeing up human agents to focus on more complex tasks. My read is that this could be a game-changer for tax preparation, making it more efficient and accurate.

Also in the news, Salesforce has rolled out a new Slackbot AI agent, transforming it from a simple notification tool into a fully powered AI agent. This move is part of Salesforce's battle for dominance in the workplace AI space, competing with the likes of Microsoft and Google. It's interesting to see how these tech giants are vying for control of the AI-powered workplace.

Another aspect of this Slackbot upgrade is its potential to change how we interact with workplace tools. Imagine having a virtual assistant that can help you prioritize tasks, answer questions, and even make recommendations. It's an exciting space to watch, and I'll be keeping an eye on how it evolves.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.

sources.title: Building self-improving tax agents with Codex
Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
